skill.name: wiki-architect
skill.description: Analyzes code repositories and generates hierarchical documentation structures with onboarding guides. Use when the user wants to create a wiki, generate documentation, map a codebase structure, or understand a project's architecture at a high level.
skill.summary: Analyzes code repositories and generates hierarchical documentation structures with onboarding guide...
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged external command, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found only Markdown prose and inline schema examples. No executable code, shell invocation, cryptographic implementation, network access, or prompt injection attempt was found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Inline Markdown Classified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Lines 26 and 60 use backticks only for Markdown inline examples such as file references and output field names. They do not instruct the model to execute shell commands or run untrusted input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is inside documentation instructions and Markdown inline code. No command syntax, interpreter call, or execution instruction appears at these locations.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Prose Classified as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Lines 3, 8, 32, and 35 describe documentation architecture, onboarding content, diagrams, and design tradeoffs. No cryptographic algorithm or security primitive is implemented or recommended.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines contain natural-language documentation guidance only. There is no hash function, cipher name, key handling, or executable code path.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Architecture Diagram Request Classified as Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Line 34 asks for a system architecture Mermaid diagram as part of generated documentation. This is normal repository documentation behavior, not host reconnaissance or environment discovery.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is a documentation outline for the target repository. It does not ask for operating system details, secrets, credentials, local network data, or hidden files.

content.value_statement: Teams often struggle to turn large repositories into clear onboarding and architecture documentation. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to produce a structured wiki catalogue with file-cited prompts.
actual_capabilities: Creates a hierarchical wiki catalogue for a code repository.
Defines onboarding sections for senior engineers and newcomers.
Organizes documentation into Getting Started and Deep Dive areas.
Requires section prompts to cite real repository files and line numbers.
Adapts documentation depth based on repository size.
Suggests language comparison paths for onboarding content.
limitations: It produces a documentation plan, not finished long-form documentation.
It depends on accessible repository files and readable project structure.
It does not verify that generated diagrams compile in Mermaid.
It does not execute tests or inspect runtime behavior.

use_cases.title: Plan an Engineering Wiki
use_cases.description: Generate a structured table of contents and prompts for documenting a repository.

use_cases.title: Create New Developer Onboarding
use_cases.description: Build a learning path that explains setup, architecture, terminology, and important files.

use_cases.title: Map Architecture for Review
use_cases.description: Identify layers, subsystems, and deep-dive sections for a codebase architecture review.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Wiki Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a wiki catalogue for this repository. Include Getting Started, onboarding, and file-cited prompts for each section.

prompt_templates.title: Build an Onboarding Path
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a newcomer onboarding path for this codebase. Include setup, key files, terminology, and progressive learning sections.

prompt_templates.title: Design an Architecture Catalogue
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this repository and produce a documentation catalogue organized by presentation, business logic, data access, and infrastructure layers.

prompt_templates.title: Produce a Principal-Level Deep Dive
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an advanced wiki structure for senior engineers. Include architectural insights, tradeoffs, Mermaid diagram prompts, and a prioritized reading order.

output_examples.input: Document a TypeScript service repository for new team members.
output_examples.output: A wiki outline with onboarding, setup, architecture, subsystem, and key file reference sections.

output_examples.input: Create a principal engineer guide for a backend codebase.
output_examples.output: A senior-level documentation plan with architectural insights, tradeoffs, diagram prompts, and deep-dive reading order.

output_examples.input: Map a small repository with fewer than ten files.
output_examples.output: A compact Getting Started catalogue with onboarding content and file-cited section prompts.

best_practices: Run it after the repository tree and README are available.
Ask for citations to real files so each section remains grounded.
Review the generated hierarchy before expanding sections into full pages.
anti_patterns: Do not use it as a substitute for security or dependency review.
Do not accept generic section titles when repository-specific names are available.
Do not expect it to create complete documentation without follow-up writing prompts.

faq.question: What does this skill generate?
faq.answer: It generates a structured wiki catalogue with onboarding, getting started, and deep-dive documentation sections.

faq.question: Does it write the full wiki pages?
faq.answer: No. It creates the hierarchy and prompts that can be used to write each page.

faq.question: Can it handle small repositories?
faq.answer: Yes. For repositories with ten files or fewer, it keeps the structure focused on Getting Started and onboarding.

faq.question: Does it require code execution?
faq.answer: No. The skill is instruction-only and focuses on reading repository structure and documentation.

faq.question: Why does it require file citations?
faq.answer: File citations keep generated documentation tied to real repository evidence.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: It is marked for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
